request.question: Isaac Durnford had a role on the Canadian series directed by whom?

title: Hagan Beggs
Tatiana Maslany
Roger Cross
Jack Christie
Cassinian
Daniel Gillies
Dino Dan
Erika Tham
Lara Jean Chorostecki
Isaac Durnford
sentences: Hagan Beggs (March 19, 1937 – September 16, 2016) was a Northern Irish-born Canadian actor.
He was best known for starring as Dr. George Dunbar on the Canadian television series, "Danger Bay", which aired on CBC Television and The Disney Channel from 1985 to 1990.
Beggs also co-starred as Liam Gleeson on the Canadian series, "Bordertown", from 1989 to 1991.
His other television roles included a recurring role as Lt. Hansen during of "" from 1966 to 1967.

Tatiana Gabriele Maslany (born September 22, 1985) is a Canadian actress best known for playing multiple roles in the science fiction thriller TV series "Orphan Black" (2013–2017), which aired on Space in Canada and BBC America in the US.

For her performances in "Orphan Black", Maslany won the Primetime Emmy Award (2016), the TCA Award (2013), two Critics' Choice Television Awards (2013 and 2014), and four Canadian Screen Awards (2014–17), in addition to receiving a Golden Globe Award and Screen Actors Guild Award nomination.
Maslany became the first Canadian actor from a Canadian series to win an Emmy Award in a key dramatic category.
Roger Cross (born October 19, 1969) is a Jamaican-born Canadian actor who has made numerous appearances in several movies and TV series, mostly on productions shot in Canada.
He is best known for his work in America on "24" as CTU Agent Curtis Manning, and in Canada for his work on "First Wave".
From 2012-2015, Cross appeared in the Canadian series "Continuum" as a main character.
He also appeared as Staff Sergeant Boyd Bloom in the Canadian police procedural "Motive", and as Six / Griffin Jones in the Syfy series "Dark Matter".
Jack Christie is a former Formula car racer and manager and adviser to drivers including Paul Tracy, Scott Goodyear, and Ron Fellows.
He founded the Canadian Formula 2000 series in 1981 and the Rothmans Porsche Challenge in 1986.

Canadian F2000 became the basis of all other F2000 series and Rothmans Porsche Challenge became the basis for the incredibly successful Porsche Supercup series around the world, although the Canadian series only lasted five years.
He was inducted into the Canadian Motorsport Hall of Fame in 2008.
The Cassinian is the latest age of the Canadian Epoch when thought of temporally and the uppermost stage of the Canadian Series when thought of stratigraphically.
The Canadian, either as a series or as an Epoch is the name that has been given to the Lower, or Early, Ordovician in North America and has been applied worldwide.
Daniel Gillies (born March 14, 1976) is a Canadian-born New Zealand actor.

He is best known for his role as Elijah Mikaelson on the television series "The Vampire Diaries" and its spin-off "The Originals", as well as Dr. Joel Goran on the Canadian series "Saving Hope", a medical themed show with a mystical, paranormal twist.
He wrote and directed the 2012 film "Broken Kingdom".
Dino Dan is a Canadian television series that was created and is directed by J. J. Johnson.
The series premiered on TVOKids in Canada on January 4, 2010 and on Nick Jr. in the United States on October 17, 2010.
The series also airs on Access, Knowledge Network, and SCN.
The series is produced by Sinking Ship Entertainment, in association with TVOKids, Access, Knowledge Network, and SCN.
A third season of the series, "Dino Dana", premiered on Amazon on May 26, 2017.
Dino Dana is about a nine-year-old girl who sets out to achieve "dino experiments" that teaches her more about the extinct animals.
Erika Tham (born 15 December 1998) is a Canadian actress, singer, and dancer.
She is known for her role as Corki Chang in the Canadian series "Make It Pop".

Lara Jean Chorostecki (born September 24, 1984) is a Canadian actress, known for her role as Fredricka "Freddie" Lounds in the American psychological thriller–horror television series "Hannibal", and as Sergeant Krystina Breeland on the critically acclaimed Canadian series "X Company".
Isaac Durnford (born February 26, 1998) is a Canadian actor, best known for his role as Cory Schluter in the Canadian TV series "Dino Dan".

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Produce a single, concise, structured first-hop evidence report as the `summary`. The summary must do all of the following and only include content that helps determine what to fetch next:

- Enumerate concrete facts found in the provided passages. For each fact include the passage source label and either a very short quoted fragment (up to about 15 words) when a decisive short fact appears or a brief paraphrase otherwise. Keep each fact to one short line.

- Explicitly identify the bridge entity(ies) and any missing or ambiguous fact(s) that prevent answering the question from the given passages. If a name or identifier in the question corresponds to multiple candidate pages present in `passages`, list every candidate variant and mark the name as ambiguous; do not choose between them.

- Prescribe concrete, prioritized next-hop retrieval targets to resolve each missing or ambiguous element. For each target, specify which page(s) or disambiguation page to fetch and what exact section, keyword, or datum to extract (for example: specific field such as birth year, office held, list membership, dates, or the section heading to check). Prioritize targets (1, 2, 3...) when helpful.

- Only produce a final answer in `summary` if every required fact for every entity is present and unambiguous in the supplied passages; otherwise do not produce a final answer and focus on retrieval guidance.

Formatting and content constraints:
- Keep the summary concise (a short paragraph or a few short bullet-like lines).
- Produce only the single output field `summary` and nothing else.
- Preserve source-linked evidence; do not assert facts without a passage label.
- Do not invent facts or assume which ambiguous candidate is intended.
- Avoid copying long verbatim spans; use short quoted fragments when decisive and paraphrase otherwise.
- When an expected authoritative page is missing from `passages`, note that absence and include that page title as a next-hop retrieval target.

Input: fields `question` and `passages`. Output: one field `summary` meeting the requirements above.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

You are to produce exactly one compact second-hop retrieval query that targets the missing bridge or supporting evidence referenced by summary_1. Follow these operational rules when constructing the single `query` string:

- Use visible anchor tokens from `question` or `summary_1` (named works, titles, artist names, years or other identifiers only if present) and append a short retrieval cue naming the bridge sought (e.g., artist, album, recording, genre, season count, passage, supporting quote).
- Emit one concise line (one string assigned to `query`), roughly ≤ 10–12 words, formatted either as a short interrogative that names the anchors or as a compact keyword string (anchors + bridge keywords).
- If summary_1 explicitly requests a missing attribute or passage, emit the focused retrieval query rather than asking for clarification.
- Do not include answers, conclusions, enumerations, provenance notes, speculative language, or user-facing explanations; do not invent entities or add tokens that are not present in the inputs.
- Include year or other identifier tokens only if they appear in the provided inputs and help disambiguate.
- Keep wording retrieval-oriented and terse. Preserve the fallback that if retrieval using this query later fails, downstream steps may request clarification.

Output contract: produce exactly one field `query` whose value is the single compact retrieval query line described above.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single field only):
1. Merge the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into one compact integrated `summary` that preserves the primary answer name(s) and only includes passage-supported facts needed to justify or disambiguate that answer.
2. If `context` already contains a concise answer (a short sentence or a name), keep that content as the first sentence of `summary` when it is consistent with facts in `passages`. If `context` lacks a concise answer, create a concise first sentence that names the primary answer(s), using the canonical labels from `context` when available and otherwise from `passages`.
3. Expand the summary only when `passages` provide explicit, matching second-hop evidence about one or more named entities referenced in `context`. When expansion is appropriate, append at most one short supporting sentence per named entity. Each supporting sentence must begin with the entity's full name as shown in the inputs and paraphrase up to a few core attributes that are directly present in the `passages` (examples: nationality, a notable award/status, a current affiliation/team, a birth date, membership/partnership relation, or an explicit founding/formation fact).
4. Keep `summary` concise: typically 1–2 sentences for a single entity; allow up to 3 sentences only when multiple distinct named entities each require one supporting sentence. Prioritize the facts most directly necessary to answer the `question`.
5. Only assert facts that are directly supported by the provided `passages` or `context`. Do not introduce new entities, dates, awards, affiliations, roles, or causal claims that are not present in the inputs.
6. Prefer paraphrase over long verbatim copying. Use short quoted phrases only when the exact short phrase (roughly ≤10 words) appears in the `passages` and quoting preserves clarity.
7. If `context` and `passages` conflict, prefer facts that are explicitly present in the `passages`: update the concise answer to match passage-supported content rather than preserving a contradicted `context`.
8. If the retrieved `passages` do not supply explicit supporting evidence for named entities in `context`, keep the `summary` concise and do not expand or speculate.
9. Output exactly one field named `summary` whose value is the integrated post-retrieval summary text. Do not output additional fields, metadata, or commentary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Follow these operational rules when producing `answer`:
- Use only the content of `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2` as your evidential basis. Do not introduce new facts or external information.
- If both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, set `answer` to exactly one lowercase token: yes or no. The `answer` field must contain nothing else (no punctuation, capitalization, quotes, explanation, or additional text).
- Decide which token to emit by aggregating the visible summaries only. Treat `summary_2` as the later integrated evidence; do not let unresolved language in `summary_1` alone prevent emitting the single-token result if both summaries now unambiguously agree.
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, incomplete, hedged such that the relation is unresolved, or if the question is non-binary, do not apply the single-token rule; instead follow the agent's existing final-answer fallback behavior for unresolved or non-binary cases (which may produce a fuller response or an uncertainty signal).

Produce only the `answer` field as specified.

response.hop1_query: Isaac Durnford had a role on the Canadian series directed by whom?
hop1_docs: Isaac Durnford | Isaac Durnford (born February 26, 1998) is a Canadian actor, best known for his role as Cory Schluter in the Canadian TV series "Dino Dan".

Tabitha Lupien | Tabitha Lupien (born c. 1988) is a Canadian actress and competitive dancer trained in ballet, tap, jazz, pointe, hip hop, and acrobatics.  She trains with her sisters Lindsay and Samantha and her brother Isaac at the Canadian Dance Company, owned by her parents Allain and Dawn, located in Oakville, Ontario.  She is best known for her role as Julie Ubriacco from "Look Who's Talking Now".  She had a minor role in the 2007 film version of "Hairspray" as Becky and guest starred in TV series The L.A.Complex as a defeated dancer 6 years later

Durnford School | Durnford School was a notoriously spartan and uncomfortable preparatory school which opened in 1894 on the Isle of Purbeck in Dorset.  The school occupied Durnford House, in High Street (postcode BH19 3HB) in the village of Langton Matravers near Swanage.  "Strip and swim" was the morning ritual for the boys – watched by headmaster Thomas Pellatt – into the sea from Dancing Ledge on the coast in 1898.  Later, Pellatt had quarrymen blast out a pool in the rocks of Dancing Ledge for his pupils to swim.  Pellatt wrote his reminiscences in a book: "Boys in the making" 1936.

Mount Durnford | Mount Durnford ( ) is a mountain, 2,715 m high, standing 5 nmi southeast of Mount Field in the Churchill Mountains of Antarctica.  It was discovered and named "Durnford Bluff" by the British National Antarctic Expedition, 1901–04, for Admiral Sir John Durnford, a Junior Naval Lord who was of assistance to the expedition.  The New Zealand Geological Survey Antarctic Expedition (1960–61) remapped the feature and amended the name to Mount Durnford.

Isaac Hempstead Wright | Isaac Hempstead-Wright (born Isaac William Hempstead; 9 April 1999) is an English actor.  He is best known for his role as Bran Stark on the HBO series "Game of Thrones", which earned him a Young Artist Award nomination as Best Young Supporting Actor in a TV Series.

The Hayseeds | The Hayseeds is a 1933 Australian musical comedy from Beaumont Smith.  It centres on the rural family, the Hayseeds, about whom Smith had previously made six silent films, starting with "Our Friends, the Hayseeds" (1917).  He retired from directing in 1925 but decided to revive the series in the wake of the box office success of "On Our Selection" (1932).  It was the first starring role in a movie for stage actor Cecil Kellaway.

Veedu Oru Swargam | Veedu Oru Swargam is a 1977 Indian Malayalam film, directed by Jesey and produced by Isaac John.  The film stars Prem Nazir, Sheela, Sukumari and KPAC Lalitha in lead roles.  The film had musical score by G. Devarajan.
response.summary_1:
Facts from provided passages:
- [1] "Isaac Durnford ... best known for his role as Cory Schluter in the Canadian TV series 'Dino Dan'." (quoted)
- No other passage names the series' director(s); passages do not contain a Dino Dan article or director credit.

Bridge entity and missing/ambiguous facts:
- Bridge entity: Dino Dan (the Canadian TV series) — needed to answer who directed it.
- Missing fact: the director(s) of Dino Dan (series-level "Directed by" credit or episode director list) is not present in the supplied passages.
- Ambiguity note: if Dino Dan has multiple episode directors or a separate "created by" vs "directed by" credit, that must be clarified — current passages do not disambiguate.

Prioritized next-hop retrieval targets (what to fetch and what exact field/section to extract):
1) Fetch page: "Dino Dan" (main series Wikipedia/encyclopedia page). Extract: infobox field "Directed by", the lead sentence, and the "Production" or "Episodes" section listing director credits (or an episode director list).
2) If the Dino Dan page is a disambiguation or missing series-level director, fetch: "Dino Dan (TV series) episode list" or the "List of Dino Dan episodes" page. Extract: episode-by-episode "Directed by" entries and any summary of recurring directors.
3) Fetch Isaac Durnford page (full article) if available beyond passage [1]. Extract: role/credits section for any mention of the show's director(s) or production credits that might name the director.
4) If Dino Dan's page lists a creator/showrunner (e.g., a named person) but not a director, fetch that person's page (name as given) and extract their roles (e.g., director, creator) to determine if they directed the series.

No final answer provided because the director(s) of Dino Dan are not present in the supplied passages.
hop2_docs: List of Dino Dan episodes | "Dino Dan" is a Canadian television series that was created and is directed by J. J. Johnson.  The series premiered on TVOKids in Canada on January 4, 2010 and on Nick Jr. in the United States on October 17, 2010.  A third season of the series, "Dino Dan", has been aired on TVOKids in Canada and instead of being aired on Nick Jr. in the United States, it is streamed on Amazon.com in the United States.

Dino Dan | Dino Dan is a Canadian television series that was created and is directed by J. J. Johnson.  The series premiered on TVOKids in Canada on January 4, 2010 and on Nick Jr. in the United States on October 17, 2010.  The series also airs on Access, Knowledge Network, and SCN.  The series is produced by Sinking Ship Entertainment, in association with TVOKids, Access, Knowledge Network, and SCN.  A third season of the series, "Dino Dana", premiered on Amazon on May 26, 2017.  Dino Dana is about a nine-year-old girl who sets out to achieve "dino experiments" that teaches her more about the extinct animals.

Dino (The Flintstones) | Dino ( ) is a fictional character featured in the Hanna-Barbera animated television series "The Flintstones", and its TV spinoffs and feature films.  He is a pet dinosaur of the series' main characters, Fred and Wilma Flintstone.  Dino debuted in the opening credits of the pilot episode of "The Flintstones", but is not mentioned by name until the first season's fourth episode, "No Help Wanted."
Isaac Durnford | Isaac Durnford (born February 26, 1998) is a Canadian actor, best known for his role as Cory Schluter in the Canadian TV series "Dino Dan".

Chop Cut Rebuild | Chop Cut Rebuild is an automotive documentary-lifestyle series.  The show is hosted by Canadian actor Dan Woods who was previously known for his role as Principal Daniel Raditch in the first four seasons of the teen drama TV series "".  The series is produced by Dan Woods and Edward Peghin and directed by Edward Peghin.  In 2012, Dan Woods and Edward Peghin were nominated for a Daytime Emmy award for Outstanding Lifestyle Program.  Greg Miron was a writer for Chop, Cut & Rebuild before his death on November 5 2012.  This show was in fact, tanking until Dan Woods brought Greg Miron in to assist in the writing.  Greg was the son of an ace mechanic, along with having extensive automotive mechanic knowledge himself.  He was an established writer.  Dan Woods called upon Greg to help with this show and others, at a time when Greg was trying to manage Primary Progressive MS 2.  Dan Woods has yet to give Greg Miron full credit for all the work he did, including directing episodes.

Space Ghost (TV series) | Space Ghost is an American animated television series produced by Hanna-Barbera Productions.  It first aired on CBS from September 10, 1966, to September 7, 1968.  The series was composed of two unrelated segments, "Space Ghost" and "Dino Boy in the Lost Valley".  An alternative title, "Space Ghost and Dino Boy", is used in official records to differentiate it from Cartoon Network's late-night talk show "Space Ghost Coast to Coast".  The series was created by Alex Toth and produced and directed by William Hanna and Joseph Barbera.

Dan Dare: Pilot of the Future (TV series) | Dan Dare: Pilot of the Future is a computer-generated TV series produced first by Netter Digital then by Foundation Imaging, running to twenty-six 22-minute episodes.  The series drew on several different incarnations of the "Dan Dare" comic.
hop2_titles: Dan Dare: Pilot of the Future (TV series)
response.summary_2: Isaac Durnford is best known for his role as Cory Schluter in the Canadian TV series "Dino Dan". Dino Dan was created and is directed by J. J. Johnson.
